title: Husbands
content:
There is gladness in his gladness, when he's glad,
     There is sadness in his sadness, when he's sad;
     But the gladness in his gladness,
     Nor the sadness in his sadness,
     Isn't a marker to his madness when he's mad.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Husbands", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
